definitions.definition: A written order by a drawer in favor of a drawee to pay a certain sum of money to a payee on a specified date. It is a negotiable instrument.
The person who draws a bill of exchange, i.e., the person who writes the bill and orders the drawee to pay the payee.
The person in whose favor a bill of exchange is drawn, i.e., the person who is ordered to pay the payee. He is also called the acceptor.
The person who is to receive the amount from the drawee on the due date of the bill of exchange.
The act of the drawee in accepting the bill of exchange by signing his name on it, thereby making himself liable to pay the amount on the due date.
The date on which the drawer draws the bill of exchange.
The date on which the drawee is required to pay the amount to the payee.
The date on which the payment of the bill of exchange becomes due.
The date on which the payment is actually made, i.e., the date on which the amount is credited to the payee's account.
The difference between the face value of the bill of exchange and its discounted value, i.e., the amount of interest for the period between the date of discounting and the due date of the bill.
The rate at which the bill of exchange is discounted, i.e., the rate at which the banker deducts the discount from the face value of the bill.
A bill of exchange which is payable to bearer, i.e., to the person who holds the bill.
A bill of exchange which is payable to a specific person, i.e., to the person whose name is mentioned on the bill.
The return of a cheque or bill of exchange by the bank to the drawer due to insufficient funds in the drawer's account.
The formal objection made by the banker or drawee to a bill of exchange which is dishonored due to insufficient funds in the drawer's account.
The act of paying off a bill of exchange before its due date, i.e., before the date of maturity.
A bill of exchange which can be transferred from one person to another by endorsement and delivery.
The act of the holder of a bill of exchange in signing his name on the back of the bill, thereby transferring the ownership of the bill to another person.
The person who legally owns a bill of exchange.
The person who acquires a bill of exchange for value, in good faith, and without notice of any defect in the title of the previous holder.
